name: ZeroMQ Distributed Messaging Library
what_it_does: Open-source asynchronous messaging library — enabling developers to build distributed and concurrent applications with socket-like message passing (pub/sub, push/pull, request/reply, dealer/router patterns), with bindings for 40+ programming languages, no broker requirement, microsecond latency, and deployable across threads, processes, and networks, used as a foundation for trading systems, HPC, and high-throughput messaging.
use_cases: Building pub/sub messaging between microservices via ZeroMQ for high-throughput event distribution
Implementing request/reply RPC patterns via ZeroMQ for low-latency inter-process communication
Distributing work across parallel workers via ZeroMQ push/pull for task queue processing
Building custom message broker via ZeroMQ patterns for specialized messaging needs
High-frequency trading messaging via ZeroMQ for microsecond-latency financial data distribution
not_for: Teams needing managed message broker with persistence (use RabbitMQ, Kafka, or NATS JetStream)
Cloud-native event streaming with replay and consumer groups (use Apache Kafka)
Guaranteed message delivery with acknowledgments (ZeroMQ is fire-and-forget by default)
best_when: A developer needs an ultra-low-latency messaging library for custom distributed system patterns — where ZeroMQ provides broker-free socket-level messaging with microsecond performance and flexible topology patterns.
avoid_when: You need message persistence, guaranteed delivery, dead-letter queues, or a managed broker service with monitoring.

auth.notes: Library, not a service — no authentication by default. Optional CURVE encryption (ZeroMQ 4.x+) for endpoint authentication and encryption using elliptic curve cryptography. PLAIN auth option (username/password — not for production). GSSAPI/Kerberos support. Auth implemented via ZMQ_CURVE_PUBLICKEY/SECRETKEY socket options. No central broker means no central auth — each endpoint manages its own auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; infrastructure costs only
pricing.notes: Created by iMatix Corporation (Pieter Hintjens, Martin Sústrik). LGPL v3 license (can link from proprietary code). Fork: nanomsg (by Martin Sústrik), later NNG (nanomsg-next-generation). ZeroMQ Foundation (non-profit). 40+ language bindings. High-performance C library (libzmq). Patterns: PAIR, PUB/SUB, PUSH/PULL, REQ/REP, DEALER/ROUTER, XPUB/XSUB. Used by: Samsung, AT&T, NASA, CERN, Los Alamos, Microsoft, LinkedIn (Kafka built partially inspired by ZeroMQ concepts). Celery distributed task queue uses ZeroMQ as backend option. No broker — nodes communicate directly. In-process (inproc://), IPC, TCP, multicast transports.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — bounded by hardware; millions of messages/second possible

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Library, not an API service — integrates as code dependency. No REST API. 40+ language bindings. No broker required. CURVE encryption for security. Self-managed topology. No webhooks. Excellent for agents needing ultra-low-latency IPC or network messaging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Library with no built-in message deduplication. Application-level idempotency required. PUB/SUB: subscribers may miss messages (no persistence). PUSH/PULL: messages not acknowledged by default.
known_agent_gotchas: NOT a service — ZeroMQ is a library that agents embed, not an API to call
No message persistence by default — if subscriber is down, messages are lost (PUB/SUB)
No message acknowledgment by default — PUSH/PULL has no delivery confirmation
CURVE encryption must be explicitly configured — insecure by default over TCP
Broker-free design means no central monitoring point — implement your own observability
Context must be managed carefully — one zmq.Context() per process, sockets not thread-safe

security_score_components.security_notes: CURVE encryption (elliptic curve) for transport security — must be explicitly enabled. LGPL open source — auditable. No default auth — insecure out of box. User responsible for all security implementation. CURVE uses ZMQ_CURVE_PUBLICKEY/SECRETKEY. No central broker reduces single point of compromise. User-controlled data residency.
